records.ety: [Perh. orig., to dress in a foolish manner, and allied to dizzy: but cf. also OE. dysyn (Palsgrave) to put tow or flax on a distaff, i. e., to dress it. Cf. Distaff.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dizened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dizening.]

defs: To dress gaudily; to overdress; to bedizen; to deck out.
quotes: Like a tragedy queen, he has dizened her out.
To-morrow when the masks shall fall That dizen Nature's carnival.
